channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Designing HPC Cluster Networking: What Speeds You Actually Need
item.description: Designing HPC Cluster Networking: What Speeds You Actually Need When building or scaling an HPC cluster, CPUs and GPUs usually get most of the attention. But in practice, the network design is...

item.title: How to Build a Vendor-Agnostic Logger (with a Grafana Implementation)
item.description: The Problem: Why I Built My Own Logger If you’ve ever tried to implement proper logging in a frontend application whether it’s React, React Native, Vue, or any other framework, you might feel the...

item.title: I Replaced $800/mo in API Costs with a Local Llama 4 Setup for E-Commerce
item.description: My team runs an e-commerce operation that pushes around 80,000 product descriptions through LLMs every month. We were spending $800+ on GPT-4o API calls. Last month we moved the bulk generation...

item.title: Microsoft offers buyouts up to 7% of US employees
item.description: Article URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/23/microsoft-offers-buyout-for-up-to-7-of-u-s-employees/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47881805 Points: 15 # Comments: 15

item.title: Introducing WebhookRelay: A Modern .NET Open Source Webhook Management Platform
item.description: Building a webhook relay infrastructure with ASP.NET Core 8 and React 19 Introduction I'm excited to announce WebhookRelay — a self-hosted, open-source webhook management platform built...

item.title: Dev Tools para Todos
item.description: Como desarrolladores, no todo lo podemos resolver escribiendo código. Algunas veces hay cierto tipo de problema que se resuelve con una herramienta externa. Y no hablo de integraciones con APIs de...

item.title: Congrats to the April Fools Challenge Winners!!
item.description: We asked you to build something completely useless and you delivered. The DEV April Fools Challenge brought out some of the most hilariously over-engineered, magnificently pointless, and genuinely...

item.title: 💻 Learning Cybersecurity by Building- A Hacker Terminal Game in Python
item.description: 🚀 Introduction (The "Why") As I started learning cybersecurity, I realized that just reading concepts or watching tutorials wasn't enough. Topics like password security, encryption, and system...

item.title: Claude Code Felt Off for a Month. Here Is What Broke.
item.description: For about four weeks in March and April, Claude Code felt noticeably worse. I was not imagining it. Sessions got repetitive. Tool calls chose weird paths. My usage limits emptied faster than the...

item.title: How My Coworker Who Didn't Know 'cd' Shipped to Production
item.description: This morning, our Design Lead asked me how to get her terminal into the right folder. Tanya [6:57 AM] my ghost isnt in tiger den anymore! GAH do i do /tiger-den or what was the command to make it...

item.title: Sonos’ big sale on refurbished speakers is about to end
item.description: Portable audio and home theater upgrades can be very pricey, which is why we make a habit out of telling readers when there are good deals happening. With that in mind, here’s a friendly reminder...

item.title: Meta is laying off 10 percent of its staff
item.description: Meta is planning to layoff around 10 percent of employees in May, according to a memo from the company's chief people officer, Janelle Gale, published by Bloomberg. That means approximately 8,000...

item.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
item.description: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

item.title: Apple’s Next Chapter, SpaceX and Cursor Strike a Deal, and Palantir’s Controversial Manifesto
item.description: In this week’s episode of Uncanny Valley, we talk about Tim Cook’s legacy as CEO at Apple and what his long-rumored departure means for the future of one of the world's biggest companies.

item.title: ‘We Are Xbox’: read the memo defining Microsoft’s gaming future
item.description: Microsoft's new Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has laid out a new strategy for what she describes as a "return of Xbox." In a joint memo with Xbox Chief Content Officer Matt Booty, Sharma and Booty lay out...

item.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox chief is ‘reevaluating’ exclusive games
item.description: Xbox CEO Asha Sharma held an all-hands meeting this morning and laid out her strategy for a "return of Xbox" with employees. While rebranding Microsoft Gaming back to Xbox was part of the agenda,...

item.title: Democrats want to ban ICE from turning warehouses into detention centers
item.description: A bill introduced by Rep. Rashida Tlaib (D-MI) would prohibit the Department of Homeland Security from converting warehouses and similar buildings into immigrant detention centers, an attempt to...

item.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
item.description: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

item.title: Spirit is broken
item.description: The bright yellow livery of Spirit Airlines may soon disappear from the skies. The country's seventh-largest airline has been in financial trouble for years: It hasn't turned a profit since 2019...

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos breach was humiliating
item.description: Anthropic's tightly controlled rollout of Claude Mythos has taken an awkward turn. After spending weeks insisting the AI model is so capable at cybersecurity that it is too dangerous to release...

item.title: At 'AI Coachella,' Stanford Students Line Up to Learn From Silicon Valley Royalty
item.description: CS 153 has gone viral on the Palo Alto campus—and on X. Not everyone is happy about it.

item.title: UNC6692 Impersonates IT Helpdesk via Microsoft Teams to Deploy SNOW Malware
item.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster known as UNC6692 has been observed leveraging social engineering tactics via Microsoft Teams to deploy a custom malware suite on compromised...

item.title: OpenAI says its new GPT-5.5 model is more efficient and better at coding
item.description: OpenAI just announced its new GPT-5.5 model, which the company calls its "smartest and most intuitive to use model yet, and the next step toward a new way of getting work done on a computer."...

item.title: Ikea’s new inflatable chair doesn’t look like an inflatable chair
item.description: Ikea shared a sneak preview of three pieces from a new experimental collection, set to be fully revealed at an annual company event on May 13th. One of the pieces is an inflatable chair that looks...

item.title: Great news, the Moto G Stylus is no longer teeming with bloatware
item.description: The 2026 edition of Motorola's stylus phone is plenty appealing. My review unit is a charming lilac color, has a pleasantly textured back panel, and includes a MicroSD slot and what might be the...

item.title: Era raises $11M to build a software platform for AI gadgets
item.description: Era thinks that we will see many form factors of AI hardware, including glasses, rings, and pendants.

item.title: Grab a ticket today: The first StrictlyVC of 2026 kicks off in just a week in San Francisco
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco is in just a week. Now’s the time to grab yourself a ticket. Join VCs and founders at Sentro Filipino Cultural Center on April 30.

item.title: Another customer of troubled startup Delve suffered a big security incident
item.description: TechCrunch has confirmed that Delve was the compliance company that performed the security certifications for Context AI, the AI agent training startup that last week disclosed a security incident.

item.title: Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign
item.description: Bitwarden CLI has been compromised as part of the newly discovered and ongoing Checkmarx supply chain campaign, according to new findings from JFrog and Socket. "The affected package version...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: $290M DeFi Hack, macOS LotL Abuse, ProxySmart SIM Farms +25 New Stories
item.description: You scroll past one incident and see another that feels familiar, like it should have been fixed years ago, but it still works with small changes. Same bugs. Same mistakes. The supply chain is...
